title: lonchocarpus mutans
intro: Lonchocarpus mutans, also known as the "Toxic Arrow Poison" plant, is a species of plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its toxic seeds and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Lonchocarpus mutans include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and ointments, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxicity.
